 Fixed Deposit – Policy & Reference

 Grievance redressal, service levels, and glossary
 Document Code: FD-POL-04  |  Version 1.0  |  June 2026
 Fixed Deposit Servicing Knowledge Base

Fixed Deposit Policy & Reference Guide
Page 2
Internal Reference – Customer Service
This guide consolidates the grievance redressal policy, service-level turnaround times across request
types, and a glossary of FD-related terms. It is intended as a quick reference for customer service teams
and for systems that prioritise or escalate depositor emails.
1. Grievance Redressal Policy
Every depositor communication, regardless of channel, is acknowledged and tracked through to
resolution. The escalation levels below apply once a request has not been resolved within its standard
turnaround time, or where the depositor has written in more than once about the same unresolved issue.
Level
Trigger
Owner
Response TAT
Level 1 – Front-line
First contact on any request
Customer Service
Executive
Acknowledge within 24
hours; resolve within the
SOP's standard TAT
Level 2 – Escalation
Unresolved past standard TAT, or
depositor's second follow-up on the
same issue
Team Lead / Senior
Executive
Resolve or provide a firm
resolution date within 3
working days
Level 3 – Grievance
Redressal Officer
Unresolved past Level 2 TAT, or
depositor explicitly requests escalation
Grievance Redressal
Officer
Resolve or respond with a
detailed status within 7
working days
Level 4 – Nodal /
Ombudsman referral
Unresolved after Level 3, or depositor
indicates intent to approach an external
forum
Nodal Officer
As per the applicable
regulatory timeline
Principles for handling repeat or multi-issue emails
●
Where a single email raises more than one distinct issue, each issue is logged and tracked separately,
and the email is not closed until every issue within it is resolved.
●
A depositor's reference to having "already written" or "written multiple times" should trigger a check for
open or previously closed cases on the account before responding.
●
Tone indicating financial distress, urgency (e.g., a wedding or medical need), or dissatisfaction with
hold times should be acknowledged directly in the response, separate from the transactional resolution
of the request itself.
2. Service Level (TAT) Summary
Consolidated turnaround times for the request types covered in the SOP document, for quick reference.
Request type
Standard TAT
Premature withdrawal
2–3 working days after confirmation
Maturity payout
Same day, up to 2 working days
Auto-renewal reversal
2–3 working days after confirmation
Interest / payout delay
3 working days from logging
KYC update
1 day (contact details); up to 5 days (address/ID)
TDS certificate (Form 16A)
Immediate to 5 working days

Fixed Deposit Policy & Reference Guide
Page 3
Internal Reference – Customer Service
Request type
Standard TAT
NOC issuance
5 working days from confirmed closure
Death claim settlement
10 working days from complete documents
Bank account change
2 working days
Duplicate statement / receipt
2 working days
3. Glossary of Terms
Term
Meaning
Cumulative FD
Interest is compounded over the tenure and paid out as a lump sum along with the principal
at maturity.
Non-cumulative FD
Interest is paid out at chosen intervals (monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, or annually) instead of
being compounded.
Tenure
The fixed period for which the deposit is booked, after which it matures.
Maturity amount
The total amount payable at the end of the tenure – principal plus accrued interest for
cumulative deposits.
Premature withdrawal
Closing the FD before its maturity date, subject to a penalty on the interest rate.
Auto-renewal
Automatic renewal of a matured FD for the same tenure if no instruction is received from the
depositor.
TDS
Tax Deducted at Source – tax withheld on interest income above a prescribed threshold and
deposited with the tax authorities on the depositor's behalf.
Form 15G / 15H
Self-declaration forms submitted by depositors whose income is below the taxable limit, to
avoid TDS deduction (15H is for senior citizens).
Form 16A
The certificate confirming the amount of TDS deducted on interest income in a financial year.
NOC
No-Objection Certificate – confirms that a loan or facility has been fully closed with no
outstanding dues.
Nominee
The person designated by the depositor to receive the FD proceeds in the event of the
depositor's death.
KYC
Know Your Customer – the identity and address verification documentation required to open
and maintain an account.
Sweep-in / Flexi FD
An FD linked to a savings account that automatically moves surplus balance into a deposit,
and reverses the sweep if funds are needed.
Bulk deposit
A deposit above a specified high-value threshold, generally priced on a separate, negotiated
rate card.
